NVDA.reasoning: 1. MACRO REGIME: Transitional leaning risk-on. S&P 500 up +9.3% in April (best month since 2020) driven by mega-cap AI earnings (Alphabet +7%, Amazon +4%). Fed held at 4.25-4.50% for third consecutive meeting (historic 8-4 dissent); zero cuts priced through 2026-2027. Higher-for-longer firmly in place. Fragile backdrop: Iran/Strait of Hormuz blockade elevating Brent toward $90/bbl, core PCE at ~2.7%, and Warsh succession adding policy uncertainty. The AI capex supercycle is the dominant risk-on driver and directly benefits NVDA — the regime wind is at this trade's back despite macro headwinds elsewhere. 2. RELATIVE STRENGTH: NVDA is a strong sector outperformer. 21d ROC at +26.69% vs S&P ~+9.3% monthly gain — nearly 3x the broad market. 5d ROC +3.33%, 10d ROC +5.22%. All EMAs fully aligned in uptrend (EMA5 through EMA200). ADX at 54.67 (strong trend) with +DI 39.03 vs -DI 4.66. Price holds above all moving averages. This is a relative-strength leader by a wide margin, consistent with institutional sector-rotation inflows into AI hardware. 3. ASYMMETRIC R/R: Entry ~$210.00 on stabilization above hourly volume-confirmed support $208.20. Target: $231.13 (daily Fib 1.272 extension from $164.27-$216.83 swing) = +10.1% upside. Stop: $204.00 (below daily EMA-10 at $204.98 and Fib 23.6% at $204.43, invalidating relative-strength thesis if broken) = -2.9% downside. Asymmetry: 10.1% / 2.9% = 3.5:1, comfortably above the 2:1 minimum and approaching the 3:1 preferred threshold. 4. CAPITAL PRESERVATION: NVDA balance sheet shows near-zero leverage per wiki thesis — no concern if conditions tighten. ATR-14 at $5.87 (2.81% daily) is elevated but regime-consistent for a high-beta AI name in risk-on. No leverage flag triggered. CAUTION FLAGS: Daily RSI-14 at 74.76 and RSI-21 at 81.52 are overbought — however hourly RSI-21 has reset to 51.94 (neutral), reducing immediate reversal risk. Hourly OBV is trending down and hourly MACD histogram is negative (-0.5885), suggesting near-term distribution/consolidation rather than fresh buying. Volume was below average on Apr 29 (0.84x). The 3-day pullback from $216.83 to $209.25 looks like a healthy pause in the uptrend rather than distribution, but entry discipline matters: wait for hourly stabilization above $208.20 support before committing. Wiki thesis confirms NVDA has been a consistent buy-on-pullback-to-EMA10 trade across 5 prior runs. Sector rotation cycle appears mid-stage — not late — given the fresh 26d momentum burst.
